reviewer.name: OpenAI Codex
reviewer.responsibility: Claim extraction, primary-source mapping, scope correction, and verification design

sources.title: Document Object Model (DOM)
sources.versionScope: Current DOM standard and evergreen browsers

claims.statement: The DOM represents a document as a node tree exposed through programming interfaces.
Document and Element APIs can locate nodes and change text, attributes, classes, or structure.
DOM operations affect the live document representation and selectors may fail to find an element.

verifications.method: Select a known element and confirm the lookup returns the expected node.
verifications.expectedResult: The lookup returns the expected node.

verifications.method: Change one text value, attribute, or class and observe the corresponding page update.
Check the missing-element path so a failed selector is visible rather than silently guessed.
verifications.expectedResult: The check exposes whether dom behaves as documented: Check the missing-element path so a failed selector is visible rather than silently guessed.

limitations: The DOM is the browser's current document representation, not an automatic edit to the source HTML file.
Selector methods can return no match; code must handle that path instead of assuming an element exists.
DOM availability and timing depend on the host environment and document lifecycle.
Independent human review remains required before promotion to gold.
